mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength and improving corporate revenue prospects, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term as earnings estimates are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth is a primary indicator of economic cycle health.
failure_condition: The signal's rolling 36-month rank information coefficient with 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 for a sustained period (more than 12 months).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL rises above a critical threshold, equity markets price in the expectation of contractionary monetary policy from the Federal Reserve, increasing the discount rate on future earnings and causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Markets are forward-looking and react to anticipated Fed policy shifts.
failure_condition: In a walk-forward test, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the inflation trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of slowing retail sales growth (RSAFS) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment. This combination compresses corporate profit margins and dampens consumer sentiment, leading to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS is negative while 3-month change in CPIAUCSL is positive
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a period of poor equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative mean 60-day forward return (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, a rapid and sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its cycle peak provides strong confirmation of a durable economic recovery, boosting investor confidence and driving positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE has declined > 1.0pp from its 12-month high
evidence_refs: fact:The 2009-2011 period showed equity market recovery alongside falling unemployment.
failure_condition: Periods meeting the trigger condition do not show a statistically significant higher mean 20-day return compared to periods that do not meet the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a strengthening supply side and robust business activity. This is a coincident indicator of economic health that supports corporate revenues and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO exhibits a rolling 36-month correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains anchored at a low level, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. Markets anticipate a future, more aggressive tightening cycle, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Context of ZIRP and rising commodity prices in 2010-2011.
failure_condition: Triggered periods do not lead to negative 60-day median returns over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A severe year-over-year contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown, signaling weakness in construction, consumer wealth, and durable goods spending, which precedes negative equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing collapse preceded the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong job creation (PAYEMS) and low absolute unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tight labor market and a confident consumer. This robust fundamental backdrop supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average change in PAYEMS > 150 and UNRATE < 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Late 1990s expansion featured both strong job growth and low unemployment.
failure_condition: The information ratio of a strategy based on this signal is less than 0.3 over a full business cycle.
